context: Mobile Web App for Field Workers (Construction/On-Site)
design_philosophy: Brutalist Utility. Optimize for bright sunlight, dirty hands, limited literacy, and cognitive fatigue. OAPS (One Action Per Screen).

spacing_and_layout.container: max-w-[390px] mx-auto w-full min-h-screen bg-[#FAFAFA] relative shadow-2xl overflow-hidden

concepts.name: Concept 1: The 'One-Task-At-A-Time' Wizard
concepts.description: Focuses the user completely on a single pending drawing. Eliminates navigation confusion by removing the 'queue' entirely from the primary view. They only see what's next.
flow: Screen 1: Full-screen Drawing Preview with floating 'Review Next Drawing' pill at the bottom.
Screen 2: Drawing pinned to top 40% of screen. Bottom 60% holds two massive buttons stacked vertically: 'Approve Drawing (Green)' and 'Reject Drawing (Red)'.
Screen 3 (If Reject): The screen turns red-tinted. A giant 120px Microphone button sits in the center. 'Tap to record reason'. A secondary 'Add Site Photo' button sits below it. No keyboard shown.
concepts.key_ui_features: Oversized action area. No traditional top-nav back buttons. Voice-first rejection bypasses the keyboard.

concepts.name: Concept 2: The 'Big Card Queue'
concepts.description: A scrollable list of pending tasks designed for gloved hands. Each task is a massive card. Tapping opens a bottom-sheet drawer to take action.
flow: Screen 1: Vertical list. Each card is 140px tall, showing a blueprint thumbnail, the floor/dept in huge text, and a pulsing 'Review Pending' badge.
Screen 2: Tapping a card opens a Bottom Sheet (Drawer) covering 90% of the screen. Drawing at the top. Huge Approve/Reject buttons sticky at the bottom.
Screen 3 (If Reject): Bottom sheet replaces buttons with a Voice Recorder UI (pulsing waves) and Camera toggle. A 'Submit' button appears only after audio is recorded.
concepts.key_ui_features: Bottom-sheet driven to keep interactions in the 'thumb zone'. Scrolling list gives a sense of remaining workload.

concepts.name: Concept 3: The 'Tinder-Style' Swipe UI
concepts.description: Turns the review process into a physical gesture, which is easier to execute without precise tapping.
flow: Screen 1: A stack of cards taking up 85% of the screen. Card shows the blueprint and floor name. Swipe Right to Approve (Green overlay icon). Swipe Left to Reject (Red overlay icon).
Screen 2: If Swiped Left (Reject), an immediate full-screen modal pops up: 'Why rejecting?' with a pulsing microphone icon. Voice note is auto-transcribed.
concepts.key_ui_features: Gamified but highly efficient. Zero small buttons. Physical gesture correlates directly to action.

components.name: Actionable Bottom Bar
components.rules: Fixed at bottom, pb-8 (for iOS safe area), px-6. Contains stacked buttons with min-h-[72px].

components.name: Voice Input Modal
components.rules: A massive 120x120px circular button with a lucide-react 'Mic' icon. Pulse animation (framer-motion) when recording. Subtext: 'Hold to speak'.

components.name: To Review Card
components.rules: border-2 border-zinc-200, bg-white, p-5, rounded-2xl. Includes a 80x80 thumbnail, massive title (text-2xl), and a chevron-right to indicate tappability.

icons_and_assets.sizing: w-8 h-8 or w-12 h-12 for primary actions. Stroke width 2.5 or 3 for visibility.

images.description: Used as the mock drawing to review in the cards and full-screen view.
On-site worker profile picture for the top navigation.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build the 3 concepts as distinct selectable tabs or distinct route views in the prototype so the client can compare them.
Wrap the entire UI in a container with 'max-w-[390px] mx-auto min-h-screen relative shadow-2xl' to simulate a mobile device screen on desktop browsers.
Implement the 'Voice-first rejection UI' explicitly. It should look like a giant dictaphone/voice-note interface (pulsing recording state), bypassing a standard text-area input.
Use massive buttons (min-h-[72px]) everywhere to simulate gloved-hand touch targets.
Remove all unnecessary chrome/navigation. The UI must be brutally simple.
Use the provided image URLs for mock blueprints and avatars. Do not use generic placeholders.
